title: Event Safety Planning
description: Pre-event safety planning frameworks, risk assessments, and stakeholder coordination.

phases.title: Safety Starts Early
phases.content: Event safety planning cannot be an afterthought. Comprehensive safety planning must begin months before the event, involving all stakeholders from promoters to emergency services.

phases.title: Event Safety Planning Framework
phases.content: A systematic approach to event safety ensures all hazards are identified and controlled. Planning should begin 6 or more months before the event with initial hazard identification and stakeholder identification. At 3 months before, conduct detailed risk assessments and submit permit applications. At 1 month before, hold final safety briefings and review emergency procedures. Key stakeholders include show operators, the LSO, venue management, fire department, and emergency medical services.

phases.title: Planning Checklist
phases.content:
Develop a planning checklist for:

1. A 3-day outdoor music festival
2. A one-night corporate event in a hotel ballroom
3. A touring show with 20 venues

keyTakeaways: Safety planning must begin months before the event, not days
Event Safety Plans should address all hazards: laser, crowd, weather, electrical
Coordinate with venue management, promoters, and emergency services
Define clear chain of command for safety decisions during the event
